description:
You are a site administrator who maintains a very small, flat-text “database” of users that lives in

        /home/user/site_users/users.db

    The database is colon-separated with exactly three fields per line in this order:

        username:status:email

    • “status” is always either “active” or “disabled”.  
    • Each user appears only once.
    
    Three “pending” instruction files are already present in the same directory:

        /home/user/site_users/pending_additions.txt
        /home/user/site_users/pending_deactivations.txt
        /home/user/site_users/pending_email_updates.txt

    Their formats are:

        1. pending_additions.txt
              username<space>email
           (Add every listed user **only if** the username does NOT already exist in users.db.
           New users must be inserted with status “active”.  
           If the user already exists, do nothing and record that you skipped the duplicate.)

        2. pending_deactivations.txt  
              username
           (Change that user’s status from “active” to “disabled”.
           If the user was already “disabled” you must still record the operation as “DEACTIVATED”.)

        3. pending_email_updates.txt  
              username<space>new_email
           (Replace the user’s e-mail with the supplied value.
           Perform the replacement even if the user is currently disabled.)

    Task
    ----
    1. Read users.db and apply all instructions from the three pending files, in **this exact order**:
         a. additions → b. deactivations → c. e-mail updates.
    2. Overwrite /home/user/site_users/users.db with the updated, final list.
       • Preserve the **original line order**, appending any newly-added users to the bottom in the
         order they appear in pending_additions.txt.
    3. Create (or overwrite if it already exists) the log file

           /home/user/site_users/ops_latest.log

       Append one log line per action carried out (or skipped) in the *exact chronological order*
       in which your script performs the operations.  
       Every log entry MUST have the following pipe-delimited, three-field format **with no spaces
       around the “|” character**:

           ACTION|USERNAME|INFO

           ACTION:
               ADDED          → you inserted a brand-new user
               DEACTIVATED    → you changed status to disabled
               EMAIL_UPDATED  → you changed a user’s e-mail address
               SKIPPED_EXISTING → user appeared in pending_additions.txt but is already present
           USERNAME: the affected username
           INFO:
               • For ADDED            → the e-mail you stored
               • For DEACTIVATED      → the literal text “status_changed_to_disabled”
               • For EMAIL_UPDATED    → the new e-mail you stored
               • For SKIPPED_EXISTING → the literal text “already_present”

       Example log line (do not copy, just follow the format):

           ADDED|john|john@example.com

    4. Leave the three pending_*.txt files untouched.

    Verification notes
    ------------------
    • The automated grader will open users.db and ops_latest.log to check:
        – exact file paths
        – exact character content (including newlines)  
    • Make sure there are **no extra spaces**, **no extra blank lines**, and
      the pipe “|” separator is used exactly once per log line.
    • Only the files mentioned above should be modified/created; everything else
      in /home/user/ must remain unchanged.

    The entire task can be performed with ~5–10 well-chosen shell commands
    (e.g., cut/grep/sed/awk, or a short POSIX-sh script).  Implement it however
    you like, but respect the required output formats precisely so the automated
    checker can validate your work.
truth:
<!-- Initial files present before the agent starts -->
    /home/user/site_users/ directory exists and is writable by the current user.

    1. /home/user/site_users/users.db   (initial content)
       --------------------------------
       alice:active:alice@example.com
       bob:active:bob@example.com
       carol:disabled:carol@oldmail.com
       dave:active:dave@example.com

    2. /home/user/site_users/pending_additions.txt
       -------------------------------------------
       eve eve@example.com
       frank frank@example.com
       carol carol@newmail.com

    3. /home/user/site_users/pending_deactivations.txt
       ------------------------------------------------
       bob
       frank

    4. /home/user/site_users/pending_email_updates.txt
       -----------------------------------------------
       alice alice@newdomain.com
       carol carol@newdomain.com

    ---------------------------------------------------
    Expected final state AFTER the agent completes
    ---------------------------------------------------

    A) /home/user/site_users/users.db   (final content)
       --------------------------------
       alice:active:alice@newdomain.com
       bob:disabled:bob@example.com
       carol:disabled:carol@newdomain.com
       dave:active:dave@example.com
       eve:active:eve@example.com
       frank:disabled:frank@example.com
       [file ends with a single trailing newline]

    B) /home/user/site_users/ops_latest.log   (final content)
       ------------------------------------------------------
       ADDED|eve|eve@example.com
       ADDED|frank|frank@example.com
       SKIPPED_EXISTING|carol|already_present
       DEACTIVATED|bob|status_changed_to_disabled
       DEACTIVATED|frank|status_changed_to_disabled
       EMAIL_UPDATED|alice|alice@newdomain.com
       EMAIL_UPDATED|carol|carol@newdomain.com
       [file ends with a single trailing newline]
